evals.prompt: I'm building a product catalog page in Next.js App Router. It needs: a server component page that fetches products, a client component for search/filter with debounced input, a product card grid that handles loading/empty/error states, and a 'Add to Cart' button with optimistic updates. Use TanStack Query for data fetching and Zustand for cart state.
evals.expected_output: Server component page, client search component with useDebouncedValue, TanStack Query hooks, Zustand cart store, error boundary, loading skeletons, empty state

assertions.name: server_component_default
assertions.description: Page component is a server component (no 'use client' directive) that fetches data directly

assertions.name: client_pushed_down
assertions.description: 'use client' only on interactive components (search, button), not on the page itself

assertions.name: tanstack_query_used
assertions.description: Uses useQuery/useMutation from @tanstack/react-query for data fetching, not raw useEffect

assertions.name: zustand_store
assertions.description: Cart state managed with Zustand create() store, not useState or Context

assertions.name: loading_error_empty_states
assertions.description: All three states handled: loading skeleton, error message, empty state with CTA

assertions.name: debounced_search
assertions.description: Search input uses debouncing (custom hook or setTimeout pattern)

last_run.notes: With-skill output omitted ProductGrid component file; test execution gap, not skill quality issue

evals.prompt: Build a real-time chat interface in Next.js App Router. Needs: a WebSocket connection hook that auto-reconnects, a message list virtualized for 10K+ messages using @tanstack/react-virtual, a typing indicator showing who's typing, a message input with Shift+Enter for newlines and Enter to send, and optimistic message sending (show message immediately, mark as 'sending', update to 'sent' on server ack). Use Zustand for chat state.
evals.expected_output: WebSocket hook with reconnection, virtualized message list, typing indicator component, message input with keyboard handling, Zustand chat store with optimistic updates

assertions.name: use_client_on_interactive_only
assertions.description: 'use client' on chat components, not on any page-level server component

assertions.name: websocket_custom_hook
assertions.description: Custom hook managing WebSocket connection with auto-reconnect logic

assertions.name: virtualized_list
assertions.description: Message list uses @tanstack/react-virtual or similar virtualization, not rendering all 10K messages

assertions.name: zustand_chat_store
assertions.description: Chat state (messages, typing users) managed in Zustand store

assertions.name: optimistic_send
assertions.description: New messages appear immediately with a pending/sending state before server confirms

assertions.name: loading_error_states
assertions.description: Connection lost state and reconnecting indicator handled

evals.prompt: Build a multi-step form wizard in Next.js with 4 steps. Each step has its own Zod validation schema. A progress indicator shows completion percentage. Users can navigate back to any completed step without losing data. Auto-save draft to localStorage every 30 seconds. Final step shows a review of all entered data before submission. Use React Hook Form with Zod.
evals.expected_output: Multi-step form with React Hook Form, per-step Zod schemas, progress bar, back navigation, localStorage draft persistence, review step

assertions.name: react_hook_form_with_zod
assertions.description: Uses useForm with zodResolver for validation on each step

assertions.name: step_state_management
assertions.description: Current step tracked in state, form data preserved across steps

assertions.name: draft_persistence
assertions.description: Auto-save to localStorage with interval or effect

assertions.name: back_navigation_preserves_data
assertions.description: Going back to a previous step shows the data the user already entered

assertions.name: loading_error_states
assertions.description: Submission loading state and error handling on the final step

assertions.name: review_step_shows_all_data
assertions.description: Final step renders all data from all steps for user confirmation

evals.prompt: Build an infinite scroll feed in Next.js App Router. Server component for initial data load (first 20 items). Client component for scroll-based loading using TanStack Query's useInfiniteQuery. Each post has like and bookmark buttons with optimistic updates. Pull-to-refresh on mobile. Loading skeletons at bottom while fetching next page. Empty state when no posts exist.
evals.expected_output: Server component initial load, client infinite scroll with useInfiniteQuery, intersection observer, optimistic like/bookmark, pull-to-refresh, skeletons

assertions.name: server_component_for_initial
assertions.description: Initial data fetched in a server component, passed to client component as props

assertions.name: tanstack_infinite_query
assertions.description: Uses useInfiniteQuery with getNextPageParam for pagination

assertions.name: optimistic_like_update
assertions.description: Like/bookmark updates UI immediately before server confirms

assertions.name: intersection_observer_for_scroll
assertions.description: Uses IntersectionObserver or a sentinel element to trigger next page load

assertions.name: loading_skeleton
assertions.description: Skeleton components shown while next page loads

assertions.name: empty_state
assertions.description: Empty state with message shown when no posts exist

evals.prompt: Build a dashboard page in Next.js App Router with 4 chart widgets using Recharts. Each widget fetches its own data independently via TanStack Query. The widget grid supports drag-to-reorder (persist order in localStorage). Use ISR with revalidate for data freshness. Each widget has its own error boundary so one widget failure doesn't crash the whole dashboard. Show skeleton loaders per widget while data loads.
evals.expected_output: Server component page with ISR, per-widget TanStack Query, per-widget error boundary, drag-to-reorder with localStorage, skeletons

assertions.name: server_component_default
assertions.description: Page is a server component with revalidate for ISR

assertions.name: per_widget_error_boundary
assertions.description: Each chart widget wrapped in its own ErrorBoundary

assertions.name: tanstack_query_per_widget
assertions.description: Each widget uses its own useQuery hook with independent query key

assertions.name: loading_skeleton_per_widget
assertions.description: Each widget shows a skeleton while its data loads

assertions.name: drag_reorder_state
assertions.description: Widget order managed in state and persisted to localStorage

assertions.name: isr_or_revalidate
assertions.description: Uses revalidate option or generateStaticParams for ISR
